mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are positive in more than 60% of rising rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate signals weakening economic activity and reduced corporate earnings, driving negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 55% of instances when the unemployment rate exceeds the 4.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer spending and construction activity, generating positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid expansion in industrial production reflects strong manufacturing output and supply-side resilience, pushing equity markets higher over the following week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative in more than 60% of recovery-phase observations, or the signal shows no predictive power outside the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price index readings trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which suppresses equity valuations and yields negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in over 55% of periods when inflation exceeds the 320 threshold, invalidating the tightening fear mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll employment growth combined with a stable unemployment rate indicates a healthy labor market that supports corporate revenues and drives positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict positive returns in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real gross domestic product confirms expanding economic demand, which boosts corporate profits and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative in more than 55% of GDP expansion periods, or the signal loses predictive accuracy in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside rising inflation creates a stagflationary environment that erodes corporate margins and triggers negative equity market performance.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in over 60% of stagflationary observations, or the interaction term shows no predictive power during late-cycle phases.
